title: ficus hirta subsp. roxburghii
intro: Ficus hirta subsp. roxburghii, commonly known as the Roxburgh's fig, is a species of fig tree native to the Himalayan region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The Roxburgh's fig is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of Ficus hirta subsp. roxburghii include teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
